title: Endoscopic instrument and disinfection system
abstract: The present disclosure provides endoscopic instruments and energy-based disinfection systems and methods for use with these instruments. A disinfection system for use with an endoscopic instrument includes a catheter having an elongate shaft with a distal end configured for advancement through a lumen within the endoscopic instrument, at least one energy transmission element disposed on the elongate shaft and a power source coupled to the energy transmission element. The power source is configured to generate energy at the energy transmission element sufficient to disinfect at least a portion of the lumen of the endoscopic instrument. The power source and the energy transmission element may be configured to generate a non-thermal plasma with sufficient energy to destroy biofilm, bacteria or other pathogens on the endoscopic instrument.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A disinfection system for use with an endoscopic instrument, the system comprising: a catheter having an elongate shaft with a distal end configured for advancement through a lumen within the endoscopic instrument; at least one energy transmission element disposed on the elongate shaft; and a power source coupled to the energy transmission element and configured to generate energy at the energy transmission energy sufficient to at least sanitize at least a portion of the lumen of the endoscopic instrument. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein the power source and the energy transmission element are configured to create a plasma at the energy transmission element, the plasma having a sufficient amount of energy to destroy at least some pathogens on the endoscopic instrument. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the plasma is a non-thermal plasma. 4. The system of claim 1 , wherein the energy transmission element is an electrode and the power source is configured to generate an electric discharge from the electrode. 5. The system of claim 4 further comprising a gas source coupled to the elongate shaft and configured to deliver a gas to the electrode. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the gas is an inert gas. 7. The system of claim 5, wherein the gas is air. 8. The system of claim 1 , wherein the power source comprises a piezoelectric transformer coupled to a source of AC voltage. 9. The system of claim 1 , wherein the energy transmission element is a light source. 10. The system of claim 1 , wherein the energy is sufficient to disinfect at least a portion 

 of the lumen of the endoscopic instrument. 11. The system of claim 9 further comprising a plurality of light sources coupled to the power source and spaced from each other along the elongate shaft of the catheter. 12. The system of claim 11 , wherein each of the plurality of light sources is configured to emit light at a different spectrum. 13. The system of claim 9, wherein the power source is configured to generate light energy at the light source at a plurality of different spectrums. 14. The system of claim 9, wherein the light source is configured to emit light within the UV spectrum. 15. The system of claim 9, wherein the light source is configured to emit light having a wavelength within a range of about 200 and 280 nanometers. 16. The system of claim 1 further comprising a programmable motor coupled to the catheter and configured to translate the elongate shaft through the lumen. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the programmable motor is configured to withdraw the catheter through the lumen a specified distance for a specified duration of time. 18. The system of claim 9, wherein the light source comprises a UV-C LED. 19. The system of claim 9, wherein the light source comprises a flexible UV-C light fiber. 20. The system of claim 9, wherein the light source is configured to emit a constant light. 21. The system of claim 9, wherein the light source is configured to emit a pulsed light. 22. The system of claim 1 further comprising a centering device coupled to the elongate shaft and configured to center at least a portion of the elongate shaft within the lumen. 23. The system of claim 1 , further comprising an annular cap at a proximal end of the elongate shaft, the annular cap having an outer diameter greater than a diameter of the elongate shaft and comprising a flexible material 24. The system of claim 23 where at least a portion of the annular cap is an absorbent material. 25. The system of claim 1 , wherein the endoscopic instrument is an endoscope. 26. An endoscopic instrument comprising: an elongate shaft having an internal lumen and a distal end portion configured to advance through a percutaneous penetration in a patient; and an energy transmission element disposed in the elongate shaft adjacent to the internal lumen and configured to emit energy at a level sufficient to at least sanitize at least a portion of the internal lumen. 27. The instrument of claim 26 further comprising a power source coupled to the energy transmission element, wherein the power source and the energy transmission element are configured to create a plasma at the energy transmission element, the plasma having a sufficient amount of energy to destroy at least some pathogens on the endoscopic instrument. 28. The instrument of claim 26, wherein the plasma is a non-thermal plasma. 29. The instrument of claim 27, wherein the energy transmission element is an electrode and the power source is configured to generate an electric discharge from the electrode. 30. The system of claim 29 further comprising a gas source coupled to the elongate shaft and configured to deliver a gas to the electrode. 31. The system of claim 30, wherein the gas is an inert gas. 32. The system of claim 30, wherein the gas is air. 33. The system of claim 27, wherein the power source comprises a piezoelectric 

 transformer coupled to a source of AC voltage. 34. The instrument of claim 26, wherein the energy transmission element is a light source. 35. The instrument of claim 26, wherein the energy is sufficient to disinfect at least a portion of the internal lumen. 36. The instrument of claim 32, further comprising a plurality of light sources spaced from each other along the elongate shaft. 37. The instrument of claim 36, wherein each of the plurality of light sources is configured to emit light at a different spectrum. 38. The instrument of claim 34 further comprising a power source coupled to the light source and configured to generate light energy at the light source at a plurality of different spectrums. 39. The instrument of claim 34, wherein the light source is configured to emit light within the UV spectrum. 40. The instrument of claim 34, wherein the light source is configured to emit light having a wavelength within a range of about 200 and 280 nanometers. 41. The instrument of claim 34, wherein the light source comprises a UV-C LED. 42. The instrument of claim 34, wherein the light source comprises a flexible UV-C light fiber. 43. A method for disinfecting an endoscopic instrument comprising: translating a catheter through an internal lumen of the instrument; and transmitting energy from an energy transmission element on the catheter at a level sufficient to disinfect at least a portion of the internal lumen. 44. The method of claim 43 further comprising generating a plasma at or around the energy transmission element, wherein the plasma is sufficient to disinfect at least a portion of the internal lumen. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the plasma is a non-thermal plasma. 46. The method of claim 44 further comprising delivering a gas to the energy transmission element and generating an electric discharge within the gas. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the gas is an inert gas. 48. The method of claim 46, wherein the gas is air. 49. The method of claim 43 further comprising emitting light energy from a light source on the catheter sufficient to disinfect at least a portion of the internal lumen. 50. The method of claim 49 further comprising emitting light energy at different spectrums from a plurality of light sources on the catheter. 51. The method of claim 49 further comprising emitting light at a plurality of different spectrums from the light source. 52. The method of claim 49, wherein the light energy is in the UV spectrum. 53. The method of claim 49, wherein the light has a wavelength within a range of about 200 and about 280 nanometers. 54. The method of claim 43 further comprising programming a motor to translate the catheter through the internal lumen a specified distance for a specified duration. 55. A disinfection system for use with an endoscopic instrument, the disinfection system comprising: a housing having an interior configured for housing the endoscopic instrument; 

 at least one energy transmission element disposed within the interior of the housing; and a power source coupled to the energy transmission element and configured to generate energy at the energy transmission energy sufficient to disinfect at least a portion of the endoscopic instrument. 56. The system of claim 55, wherein the energy transmission element is a light source. 57. The system of claim 55, further comprising a plurality of light sources coupled to the power source and spaced from each other within the interior of the housing. 58. The system of claim 57, wherein each of the plurality of light sources is configured to emit light at a different spectrum. 59. The system of claim 56, wherein the power source is configured to generate light energy at the light source at a plurality of different spectrums. 60. The system of claim 56, wherein the light source is configured to emit light within the UV spectrum. 61. The system of claim 56, wherein the light source is configured to emit light having a wavelength within a range of about 200 and 280 nanometers.
